records.ety: [AS. wiðstandan. See With, prep., and Stand.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Withstood (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Withstanding.]

defs: To stand against; to oppose; to resist, either with physical or moral force; as, to withstand an attack of troops; to withstand eloquence or arguments.
quotes: I withstood him to the face.
Some village Hampden, that, with dauntless breast. The little tyrant of his fields withstood.
